title: Practice Problem - Custom Exception in User API

content.problemStatement:
**Instructions:**
1. **Complete your code** first.
2. Click on **"Run"** to **start the server** and make sure it runs correctly.
3. If you **make any changes** to the code after running, you must **run the server again**.
4. Only after running the server with the **latest changes**, your submission will reflect the updated solution.
5. **Do not submit** if the server is not running; otherwise, your solution will **fail**.



---


**Goal :** \
Add a new API that updates the email of a user. \
If the user does not exist, the API should throw:
```sss
UserNotFoundException
```
Project Structure
```sss
src/main/java/com/example/demo
в”‚
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ controller
в”‚     в””в”Ђв”Ђ UserController.java
в”‚
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ repository
в”‚     в””в”Ђв”Ђ UserRepository.java
в”‚
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ exception
в”‚     в””в”Ђв”Ђ UserNotFoundException.java
в”‚
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ model
в”‚     в””в”Ђв”Ђ User.java
в”‚
в””в”Ђв”Ђ DemoApplication.java
```

**Your Task** \
Complete the Complete the Missing Part of code : \
Add the following endpoint in `UserController`.
```java
PUT /api/users/{id}/email
```
This API should:
- Find the user by **id**
- Update the **email**
- Save the updated user

If the user **does not exist**, throw:
```java
UserNotFoundException
```

---

**Example Request**
```java
PUT /api/users/1/email
```
Request Body
```json
{
  "email": "alice@example.com"
}
```

---

**Expected Behavior**

**Case 1: User Exists** \
Response:
```json
{
  "id": 1,
  "name": "Alice",
  "email": "alice@example.com"
}
```

**Case 2: User Does NOT Exist** \
Request:
```json
PUT /api/users/10/email
```
**Expected result:**
```sss
UserNotFoundException
User not found with id: 10
```

---

**Hint** \
Use:
```java
userRepository.findById(id)
```
If the user is not found:
```java
throw new UserNotFoundException(...)
```
